cap_growth.notes: Each offseason: new_cap = current_cap * (1 + base_rate + random_in(-variance, +variance))

salary_inflation.notes: Average FA asking price grows independently of cap to model market inflation

tv_deal_spike.notes: Spike year is randomized at game start within the cycle window. Applied once per cycle then a new cycle begins.

two_seasons_before.message_template: The NBA's current television deal expires in 2 years. League sources expect significant revenue growth when a new deal is signed. Analysts project a notable salary cap increase.

one_season_before.message_template: The NBA has entered formal negotiations with broadcast partners. Early reports suggest the new TV deal could increase league revenue by {min_pct}–{max_pct}%. The salary cap is expected to rise significantly next offseason.

spike_offseason.message_template: The NBA has finalized a landmark television deal. The salary cap will increase from ${old_cap} to ${new_cap} next season — a jump of {jump_pct}%. Expect a highly active free agency period.

world_state_keys.notes: Stored in WorldState at game start. Clock.gd checks these each new season to fire warning events.

world_state_hooks.new_season_check: Clock.gd fires _check_tv_deal_warnings() at TRAINING_CAMP_START each year
world_state_hooks.spike_application: Applied during cap_figure_announced date in offseason
world_state_hooks.notes: When news engine is built, warning events route through it. Until then, they post directly via EventBus.notification_posted.
